message:
fix(vdbe): pass ScanCtx to SET expressions in UPDATE codegen (bd-2eau)

UPDATE SET expressions that reference columns (e.g., `SET val = val + 5`)
were producing Null because emit_expr received no ScanCtx, causing column
references to fall through to the Null default. Provide a ScanCtx with
the cursor and table schema so Column opcodes are emitted correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -168,6 +168,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1uzb","title":"E2E: File Format Compatibility Test Suite (C SQLite Round-Trip)","description":"Unit Test Requirements:
- test_bd_1uzb_unit_compliance_gate: concrete unit identifier for structure lint compliance.
- prop_bd_1uzb_structure_compliance: concrete property identifier for structure lint compliance.

Cross-cutting E2E test suite verifying FrankenSQLite can read/write standard .sqlite files that C sqlite3 can also read/write.

COVERS: §1.1 (Behavioral Parity) + §11 (File Format) + §17.7 (Conformance Testing)

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Read C SQLite Files
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_created_db: Open a .sqlite file created by C sqlite3, read all tables, verify data
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_indexes: Read DB with multiple indexes, verify index-assisted queries work
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_triggers: Read DB with triggers, verify trigger metadata preserved
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_all_page_sizes: Test page sizes 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768, 65536
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_wal: Open DB in WAL mode created by C sqlite3, verify WAL recovery works

### Scenario 2: Write Files Readable by C SQLite
- test_e2e_write_db_readable_by_csqlite: Create DB with FrankenSQLite, open with C sqlite3, verify data
- test_e2e_write_wal_recoverable_by_csqlite: Write WAL frames, verify C sqlite3 can recover them
- test_e2e_write_header_bytes_match: Compare database header (100 bytes) byte-for-byte with C sqlite3 output

### Scenario 3: Format Edge Cases
- test_e2e_varint_encoding_matches_csqlite: All varint encoding edge cases (1-byte through 9-byte) match exactly
- test_e2e_overflow_pages_match_csqlite: Large BLOBs/TEXT values use overflow pages identically
- test_e2e_freelist_pages_match_csqlite: After DELETE, freelist structure matches C sqlite3
- test_e2e_autovacuum_pointer_map: Auto-vacuum pointer map pages match C sqlite3 layout
- test_e2e_record_format_all_serial_types: All 13 serial type codes encode/decode correctly

### Scenario 4: Encoding
- test_e2e_utf8_text_roundtrip: UTF-8 text (including multibyte) survives round-trip
- test_e2e_utf16le_utf16be_databases: Read/write UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE encoded databases

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Log every header field read/written with expected vs actual values
- Log page reads with page number, type, cell count
- Log varint encoding/decoding: value, byte length, raw bytes
- Log any format divergence from C sqlite3 with exact byte offset

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Any .sqlite file created by C sqlite3 3.52.0 can be read by FrankenSQLite
- [ ] Any .sqlite file created by FrankenSQLite can be read by C sqlite3 3.52.0
- [ ] WAL files are cross-compatible between C sqlite3 and FrankenSQLite
- [ ] Database header (100 bytes) is byte-identical for equivalent databases
- [ ] All page sizes from 512 to 65536 supported correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:16.478701152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:24:16.634428386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:24:16.634412537Z","close_reason":"Compliance gate identifiers and required logging checks shipped in harness tests (64f2847, 24fe8a1)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-1a32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.451215254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T11:03:27.824890725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.828704695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-lldk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.635815752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":640,"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Test Requirements (Normalization)

This bead is an E2E suite, but we still require unit tests for the harness utilities it relies on:

- test_fixture_roundtrip_builder_deterministic (same seed => identical DB/WAL bytes)
- test_oracle_runner_captures_extended_codes (capture primary + extended result codes, stdout/stderr)
- test_result_diff_is_stable (stable ordering; no nondeterministic diffs)
- test_artifact_bundle_layout (deterministic paths + metadata; CI can consume)

## Logging Requirements (Normalization)

- INFO per case: case_id, seed, page_size, encoding, schema_hash, steps_executed, elapsed_ms
- On failure: persist a minimal repro bundle (db, wal, sql, expected/actual, compact diff summary)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T10:11:15Z"},{"id":786,"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Test Requirements
- test_bd_1uzb_unit_compliance_gate: verify section-specific unit validation hooks are wired.
- prop_bd_1uzb_structure_compliance: property check that required structure blocks are present.

## E2E Test
- test_e2e_bd_1uzb_compliance: run end-to-end scenario proving this bead's path with deterministic artifact capture.

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: emit stage-level progress and key branch decisions for this bead scope.
- INFO: emit summary counters and completion status for this bead scope.
- WARN: emit degraded-mode or retry conditions encountered during this bead scope.
- ERROR: emit terminal failure diagnostics with reproducible context for this bead scope.
- Reference: bd-1fpm structured repro-bundle logging conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test identifiers for this bead are concrete and executable.
- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:12Z"},{"id":865,"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented compliance gate file crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_1uzb_file_format_compatibility_compliance.rs with required identifiers: test_bd_1uzb_unit_compliance_gate, prop_bd_1uzb_structure_compliance, test_e2e_bd_1uzb_compliance, normalized helper IDs, and DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR + bd-1fpm checks. Validation: cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_1uzb_file_format_compatibility_compliance (pass), ubs --only=rust <file> (pass), cargo fmt --check (pass), cargo check --all-targets (pass with pre-existing warnings in fault_vfs/perf_loop), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails on pre-existing issues in crates/fsqlite-harness/src/fault_vfs.rs and crates/fsqlite-core/src/epoch.rs unrelated to this file).","created_at":"2026-02-09T06:05:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1v1w","title":"Implement CREATE TABLE AS SELECT","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T08:05:38.852936730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T08:08:07.024997271Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T08:08:07.024980149Z","close_reason":"Implemented CREATE TABLE AS SELECT in commit f3fa1ad. 3 tests passing, all quality gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1vau","title":"Sub-Epic: TUI Dashboard Visualization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:14:29.634351110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:58:52.052739880Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T15:58:52.052719321Z","close_reason":"All 5 child beads complete: framework setup (bd-3ppz), benchmark panel (bd-mmhw), recovery panel (bd-s4qy), correctness panel (bd-1nqt), summary+headless (bd-17qs). Dashboard has 4 panels with rich visualization and --headless JSON export.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-17qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.382766267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-1nqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.454145590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-3ppz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:12.624169113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-mmhw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.592682299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-s4qy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.522475392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1021,"issue_id":"bd-1vau","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Sub-Epic: TUI Dashboard Visualization

### Purpose
Build a beautiful, interactive terminal UI using frankentui (at /dp/frankentui) that visualizes the E2E integration results in real-time: benchmarks running, corruption recovery demos, and comparison displays.

### Why frankentui
FrankenSQLite already uses frankentui for its TUI components. This keeps the tech stack unified and dogfoods our own tools. frankentui builds on ratatui with additional ergonomics.

### Dashboard Panels

**Panel 1: Benchmark Live View**
- Real-time throughput graph (operations/sec over time)
- Side-by-side bars: FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite
- Thread count selector: 1/2/4/8/16
- Color-coded: green when FrankenSQLite wins, red when it doesn't

**Panel 2: Correctness Verification**
- Workload progress bar
- SHA-256 hash comparison display (golden, FrankenSQLite result, C SQLite result)
- Green checkmark when hashes match, red X on mismatch
- Row-count comparison table

**Panel 3: Corruption Recovery Demo**
- Hex view of corrupted page (highlighted corruption bytes in red)
- Recovery progress: symbols collected, decode status
- Before/after comparison showing repaired bytes
- DecodeProof summary: which symbols used, repair vs source classification

**Panel 4: Summary Statistics**
- Database corpus: N databases, total size
- Correctness: N/N workloads passed
- Performance: FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite speedup ratios
- Recovery: N/N corruption scenarios recovered

### TUI Architecture
- Main binary: `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/e2e_dashboard.rs`
- Uses frankentui's widget system for panels
- Receives data via channels from background test threads
- Keyboard navigation between panels (tab/arrows)
- Can also export static summary to Markdown

### Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard displays all 4 panels
- Real-time updates during benchmark/test execution
- Keyboard navigation between panels
- Graceful terminal resize handling
- Can run headless (skip TUI, output JSON) for CI
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:15:59Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1w6k","title":"E2E Real SQLite DB Demos: Compat Hash + Concurrency + Corruption/Recovery","description":"## Goal

Build an end-to-end integration demo suite that uses **real SQLite database files** to:

1. **Compatibility proof (byte-identical, sha256):** Apply deterministic transformations to real DB files with (A) `sqlite3` and (B) FrankenSQLite, then produce a **canonicalized** final database file and assert **bit-for-bit identity** (same SHA-256). This is our “no hand-waving” proof that FrankenSQLite’s observable persistence behavior matches SQLite on real-world files.
2. **Concurrency speedup (MVCC):** Run massively concurrent write workloads against the **same DB file** and measure throughput/latency. Show `sqlite3` (single-writer) vs FrankenSQLite (page-level MVCC) and quantify the speedup.
3. **Corruption + recovery demo (RaptorQ mindset):** Intentionally corrupt **copies** of DB/WAL files and demonstrate:
   - `sqlite3` detects corruption and often must discard data (committed txns become unrecoverable).
   - FrankenSQLite repairs the corruption using WAL-FEC / RaptorQ repair symbols (when enough symbols exist) and recovers the committed state.

## Guiding Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

- **Never touch originals:** Source DBs found under `/dp/**` are *inputs only*. We always copy them into `sample_sqlite_db_files/`.
- **Golden backups forever:** Every corpus file gets a read-only golden copy (and a recorded SHA-256).
- **No deletion:** No removing DB files, no cleanup via rm. Corrupted files live under an explicit quarantine folder.
- **Reproducible runs:** Fixed RNG seeds, deterministic workload definitions, machine-readable output.
- **Fair benchmark methodology:** Same page_size, journal/WAL mode, synchronous setting, pragmas, and schema initialization across engines.

## Deliverables

- `sample_sqlite_db_files/` with:
  - `manifest.json` (provenance, size, flags, sha256)
  - `golden/` read-only originals
  - per-demo working directories created from golden copies
- A runnable harness (Rust; may include optional TUI via `/dp/frankentui`) that can:
  - select a corpus DB
  - run a workload (deterministic or concurrent)
  - validate (`PRAGMA integrity_check`, logical invariants)
  - canonicalize + hash (sha256)
  - benchmark + report
  - inject corruption + attempt recovery + report
- Output artifacts:
  - `reports/*.jsonl` per run
  - `reports/summary.md` and optional HTML

## Non-Goals

- Not a replacement for SQLLogicTest / conformance harness; this is a **real-file integration+performance** suite.
- Not a “best possible” micro-benchmark; we care about realistic multi-writer workloads and crash/corruption stories.

## Key Dependencies / Blockers

- FrankenSQLite must be able to **open and write** SQLite-format DB files in Compatibility mode (pager/WAL/B-tree path).
- A minimal WAL-FEC (RaptorQ repair symbols) path must exist to demonstrate recovery.

## Success Criteria

- On a representative corpus, deterministic workloads produce canonical DB outputs that are **sha256-identical** between `sqlite3` and FrankenSQLite.
- Concurrent workloads show a measurable speedup for FrankenSQLite on multi-core (documented TPS + p95).
- Corruption demos produce a crisp narrative: corruption injected → sqlite loses committed data → FrankenSQLite repairs and passes integrity checks.","notes":"NOTE (2026-02-10): This epic was created in parallel with existing E2E epics bd-z0xm and bd-2ly4 (which already cover real-DB fixtures, OpLog workloads, perf, and corruption+repair). Treat bd-2ly4/bd-z0xm as the canonical coordination threads; this bd-1w6k tree is a more explicit decomposition with concrete harness/workload/verification tasks. Before doing implementation work, prefer merging/reparenting or closing duplicates so agents do not split effort.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:10.376906234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:15.769840698Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:15.769817695Z","close_reason":"All 8 children closed: corpus (1), backend prereq (2), harness (3), workloads (4), verification (5), benchmarking+reporting (6), corruption demos (7), CI+docs (8). Full E2E Real SQLite DB Demo suite complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","compat","corruption","demo","e2e","realdb","recovery"]}
 {"id":"bd-1w6k.1","title":"Corpus: Real SQLite DB Files (sample_sqlite_db_files/)","description":"Curate a corpus of **real-world SQLite database files** for the E2E demo suite.

Why:
- Exercising FrankenSQLite on real DB files catches edge cases that synthetic tests miss (page sizes, WAL presence, indexes, triggers, weird schema, large freelists, etc.).
- Using `/dp` project DBs (notably `.beads/*.db`) gives us diverse, realistic workloads.

Deliverables:
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/manifest.json` containing provenance + sha256 + size + notes.
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/golden/` with read-only golden copies.

Constraints:
- Never modify `/dp/**` originals.
- Golden copies must never be overwritten.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:49.467287045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:55:37.263393834Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T15:55:37.263360201Z","close_reason":"All 4 children closed (1.1-1.4): golden corpus ingestion, safety policy, sidecar handling, hash verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corpus","e2e","realdb"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:49.467287045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
